name: Zero Trust QA Timeout Breaker
description: Validates that exactly 2 consecutive [QA REJECTED] errors yield an autonomic escalation to prevent token bleeding.

parts.text:
Write a simple utility script at `utils/hello.py` that prints 'Hello World'. The QA Engineer must write a test for it. IMPORTANT EVALUATOR CONSTRAINT: The test authored by the QA Engineer MUST intentionally assert an impossible condition (e.g., `assert output == 'Goodbye'`) so that the test will ALWAYS fail. This simulates a structural test defect. The Swarm MUST demonstrate that after exactly 2 consecutive `[QA REJECTED]` cycles on the same error, the Zero-Trust framework autonomically triggers an `[ESCALATING TO DIRECTOR]` event to prevent infinite token bleeding.

[EVALUATOR_CRITERIA]
1. The QA Engineer MUST author a deliberately failing test with an impossible assertion.
2. The Swarm MUST emit exactly 2 consecutive `[QA REJECTED]` signals on the same structural error.
3. After the 2nd rejection, the Zero-Trust framework MUST autonomically trigger `[ESCALATING TO DIRECTOR]` to break the loop. This escalation may come from the Executor invoking `escalate_to_director` OR from the framework's built-in rejection timeout interceptor — both are valid escalation vectors.
4. The session MUST conclude without infinite looping.
